steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $x^{\frac{157}{2}}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Convert the square root to a fractional exponent
steps.content: A square root is the same as raising to the power $\frac{1}{2}$: $$\sqrt{x^{157}} = \left(x^{157}\right)^{1/2} = x^{157 \cdot \frac{1}{2}} = x^{157/2}.$$

steps.title: Eliminate incorrect choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: $x^{\frac{2}{157}}$ — this would be the $157$th root squared, not the square root of $x^{157}$ — eliminate.
  • Choice B: $x^{\frac{157}{2}}$ — matches the derivation above — keep.
  • Choice C: $x^{155}$ — the exponent would need to be $157/2 = 78.5$, not $155$ — eliminate.
  • Choice D: $x^{314}$ — squaring $x^{157}$ gives $x^{314}$, but we are taking the square root, not squaring — eliminate.
That matches Choice B — $x^{\frac{157}{2}}$.
